title: Protein extraction, alkylation, and digestion for LC/MS of HEK-293
author: Shun Adachi
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.h6gb9bw

steps.instruction: Wash approximately 10 6 harvested cells once in Krebs-Ringer-Buffer (KRB; 154 mM NaCl, 5.6 mM KCl, 5.5 mM glucose, 20.1 mM HEPES pH 7.4, 25 mM NaHCO 3 ).

substeps.instruction: Wash approximately 10 6 harvested cells once in Krebs-Ringer-Buffer (KRB; 154 mM NaCl,

steps.instruction: Resuspend the cells in 30 µl of RIPA buffer (NACALAI, INC., Kyoto, Japan) , passed in and out through 21G needles for destruction, and incubate on ice for 1 h.
Centrifuge the cells at 10,000 g for 10 min at 4°C.
Collect the supernatants.
Quantify the proteins by using a Micro BCA Protein Assay Kit (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Waltham, U.S.A.).
Solidify the samples in acrylamide gel. For further steps, use XL-Tryp Kit Direct Digestion (APRO SCIENCE, Naruto, Japan).
Wash the samples in ultrapure water. (1/2)
Wash the samples in ultrapure water. (2/2)
Wash the samples in dehydration solution. (1/3)
Wash the samples in dehydration solution. (2/3)
Wash the samples in dehydration solution. (3/3)
Dry the samples.
Process the samples using an In-Gel R-CAM Kit (APRO SCIENCE, Naruto, Japan).
Reduce the samples for 2 h at 37°C.
Alkylate the samples for 30 min at room temperature.
Wash samples with ultrapure water. (1/5)
Wash samples with ultrapure water. (2/5)
Wash samples with ultrapure water. (3/5)
Wash samples with ultrapure water. (4/5)
Wash samples with ultrapure water. (5/5)
Wash samples with destaining solution. (1/2)
Wash samples with destaining solution. (2/2)
Dry the samples.
Trypsinize the resultant samples overnight at 35°C.
Collect the dissolved digested peptides by ZipTipC18 (Merck Millipore,Corp., Billerica, U.S.A.).
Dampen the tips with acetonitrile x three tips. (1/2)
Dampen the tips with acetonitrile x three tips. (2/2)
Equilibrate tips with 0.1% trifluoroacetic acid x three tips. (1/2)
Equilibrate tips with 0.1% trifluoroacetic acid x three tips. (2/2)
Collect the peptides by ~20 cycles of aspiration and dispensing x three tips.
Wash the peptides with 0.1% trifluoroacetic acidx three tips. (1/2)
Wash the peptides with 0.1% trifluoroacetic acid x three tips. (2/2)
Elute by 0.1% trifluoroacetic acid /50% acetonitrile with aspiration and dispense x three tips. (1/5)
Elute by 0.1% trifluoroacetic acid /50% acetonitrile with aspiration and dispense x three tips. (2/5)
Elute by 0.1% trifluoroacetic acid /50% acetonitrile with aspiration and dispense x three tips. (3/5)
Elute by 0.1% trifluoroacetic acid /50% acetonitrile with aspiration and dispense x three tips. (4/5)
Elute by 0.1% trifluoroacetic acid /50% acetonitrile with aspiration and dispense x three tips. (5/5)
Vacuum dry the peptides.
Store the finalized samples at −20°C.
Before performing LC/MS, resuspend samples in 0.1% formic acid.
Quantify the amounts with the Pierce Quantitative Colorimetric Peptide Assay (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Waltham, U.S.A.).
